title: SCP-4975, The Cuckoo Clock
description: Do you hear the sound of a clock? Has it stopped? Then it means your time... is up...
body:
The Cuckoo Clock adds a terrifying new monster. Its history comes from the SCP universe, where it is referred to as SCP-4975, also known as The Cuckoo Clock or Time's Up.


At first, SCP-4975 remains passive, simply observing you from a distance, producing clicking sounds made by the turning of its head. When it enters its second phase, SCP-4975 becomes hostile. You may be cursed with the Curse of the Clock effect. Once this effect is over, SCP-4975 will spawn behind you, ready to attack. If you don't run fast enough, you die. If SCP-4975 disappears or dies, it may drop a mysterious book. Be careful when opening it. It could either reset to phase one, or giving you the Curse of the Clock effect. It's your choice.

## **Updates continue on Curseforge** ##

- A mod made by Zcoper
- Mod created with MCreator!
- Requires Geckolib

● Visit my Discord Server!
[https://discord.gg/akP56NtmVQ](https://discord.gg/akP56NtmVQ)



● Important FaQ

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>



► [OLD] What is my console spaming?

Most mobs use special spawning conditions, which means that these mobs can only appear under certain conditions. If the spawning conditions work, this spam is created in the console.

► [OLD] Why can't I summon a mob?

Spawning Conditions determine which conditions must be met in order to spawn certain mobs. Some mobs only spawn above Y level 60. Some below. Most mobs can only spawn in the overworld than in other dimensions.

► Why are features missing in different versions?

It is recommended to play the newest versions of higher minecraft versions.

Not all features of MCreator available or the same in different versions.
For example: Version 1.19.2 doesn't have the feature of shield, better structure generation or custom villagers, but Version 1.20.1 does have them.

► Where does SCP-4975 spawn?

SCP-4975 can spawn anywhere in the overworld like a normal monster. However, there can only be one instance of SCP-4975.

► How does the Phases work?

In SCP-4975's phase 1, SCP-4975 only stalks the player. In phase 2, SCP-4975 attacks through the Curse of the Clock effect. 

Phase 2 can be switched on by the gamerule "chuckooClockPhase2" if this is set to true. If the gamerule is set to false, phase 1 is active.

</details>



![SCP-4975](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b68a99ef4edd0408b01131b4e93e6e0ef7045bd2.png)
SCP-4975 is a bird-like humanoid creature that produces distinct clicking sounds as it rotates its head. In the first phase of SCP-4975, it will stalk the player but vanish if the player approaches too closely. However, when SCP-4975 transitions into its second phase, the player will receive a chat message and the Darkness effect. During this phase, players might also receive the Curse of the Clock effect. If the effect expires, a message will appear in the chat saying, "Your time is up." Shortly after, SCP-4975 will spawn behind the player and attempt to kill them. SCP-4975 is capable of destroying blocks, and it is strong, fast, and dangerous. If SCP-4975 kills a player, it returns to its first phase. If the player manages to defeat SCP-4975 or make it disappear, it may drop an Old Folk Book.

 

- Config File not inculed.
- You are all allowed to use this mod in your modpacks!
- Do not port my mods without permission!
